ogTitle: Bawley Point vs Baxters Ridge: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bawley Point and Baxters Ridge.
heading: Bawley Point vs Baxters Ridge — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bawley Point, New South Wales and Baxters Ridge, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Baxters Ridge has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Bawley Point (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bawley Point or Baxters Ridge safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bawley Point has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Baxters Ridge scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bawley Point scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Baxters Ridge scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bawley Point or Baxters Ridge?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Baxters Ridge has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
